feed.title: Meta Newsroom
feed.subtitle: Meta news, engineering, and AI blogs -- Meta.com, About Meta, Engineering at Meta, and AI at Meta -- in one feed.

entry.title: Reels Is Shaping India’s Video-First Future Across Gen Z, Women & Bharat
entry.content: Findings from new Meta-commissioned studies by IPSOS show that India’s video economy has become truly nationwide, with Reels emerging as the defining short-form video platform driving discovery, culture, creator engagement and commerce across Gen Z, women and Bharat audiences. The post Reels Is Shaping India’s Video-First Future Across Gen Z, Women & Bharat appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: Leading Tech Companies and Law Enforcement Join Forces to Disrupt Criminal Scam Networks in Southeast Asia
entry.content: The operation was the first of its kind to include companies from across the industry in the fight to protect people from online scammers. The post Leading Tech Companies and Law Enforcement Join Forces to Disrupt Criminal Scam Networks in Southeast Asia appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: Be There for Every Customer With Meta Business Agent
entry.content: Meta Business Agent is AI that lets every business show up for every customer, as if they had an infinite team behind them. The post Be There for Every Customer With Meta Business Agent appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: New 13+ Content Settings for Teen Accounts Expanding Globally on Instagram, Facebook, and Messenger
entry.content: Inspired by movie ratings criteria and parent feedback, this setting is designed to help teens see age-appropriate content by default. The post New 13+ Content Settings for Teen Accounts Expanding Globally on Instagram, Facebook, and Messenger appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: What to Play on Meta Quest: May 2026 Recap
entry.content: Each month, we round up all the biggest new releases and content updates so you can keep up with what’s happening on Meta Quest and what’s pushing the envelope in VR.

entry.title: New Games Join the Meta Horizon+ Catalog in June
entry.content: Rhythm, mystery, and a whole lot of personality — June’s Meta Horizon+ lineup has something for everyone.

entry.title: Take Flight and Explore the Unknown Skies of 'Compass,' Launching Today on Meta Quest
entry.content: ‘Compass’ is an open-world VR flying adventure from Montreal studio TREBUCHET, the team behind Broken Edge, Winds & Leaves, and Prison Boss VR.

entry.title: Protecting Players and Fans during FIFA World Cup 2026™
entry.content: We're sharing information about our efforts to combat scams and reduce abuse on our apps during the FIFA World Cup 2026™. The post Protecting Players and Fans during FIFA World Cup 2026™ appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: Boz to the Future Episode 26: The Future According to Ed Catmull
entry.content: In this episode, Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth sits down with Ed Catmull, co-founder of Pixar, former president of Disney Animation, and one of the most influential figures in the history of computer graphics.

entry.title: ‘Beat Saber’ Celebrates Eight Years With Three Free Songs, Featuring a New In-House Track
entry.content: It’s time to cue the beat, lock into the rhythm, and slash notes to some heavy ‘Beat Saber’ tech-dance bass drops — with three free songs to play

entry.title: SilverTorch: Index as Model — A New Retrieval Paradigm for Recommendation Systems
entry.content: We’re introducing SilverTorch, a reimagining of recommendation systems that unifies all retrieval components for user generated content under a unified architecture. SilverTorch shows up to 23.7x higher throughput compared to the state-of-the-art approaches. It’s also showing 20.9x more compute cost efficiency compared to a CPU-based solution while also improving accuracy. Our research paper, “SilverTorch: A [...] Read More... The post SilverTorch: Index as Model — A New Retrieval Paradigm fo...

entry.title: Our AI Wearables Are “Changing the Game” for Disabled People
entry.content: We're rolling out new features on our AI glasses that make the technology more accessible to people with disabilities. The post Our AI Wearables Are “Changing the Game” for Disabled People appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: From The Met to the World: Discover the Stories of Oceania in a New Virtual Exhibition
entry.content: For a year, The Metropolitan Museum of Art has featured 650 extraordinary artworks from across Oceania. Now, the ‘Oceania: A New Horizon of Space and Time’ experience — available on Meta Quest and desktop — is bringing the physical show’s spirit to anyone who can’t visit in person.

entry.title: Introducing Business AI on WhatsApp for Small Businesses in India
entry.content: To enable small businesses with AI-powered customer support directly into the WhatsApp Business app, we've launched Business AI in India. The post Introducing Business AI on WhatsApp for Small Businesses in India appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: Six Months of Meta Ray-Ban Display
entry.content: It’s been a little over six months since we launched Meta Ray-Ban Display — our most advanced AI glasses ever built — and it’s been awesome to see the response from our community.

entry.title: The Goo Goo Dolls Bring Their Iconic Sound to Meta Quest in a New VR Concert
entry.content: The Goo Goo Dolls have been rocking audiences for 40 years, but now you can see them on stage as you never thought possible on Meta Quest. The Goo Goo Dolls from the Beacon Theatre is an immersive concert that’s available for free on Horizon TV.

entry.title: Step into Summer: Save Big on Meta's Collection of AI Glasses
entry.content: Summer’s almost here and Meta’s AI glasses are on sale starting at just $224 for a limited time.

entry.title: From Scroll to Chat to Cart: Trends Reshaping How India Shops
entry.content: On the sidelines of the 2026 Meta Marketing Summit E-Commerce edition, we are sharing key trends shaping the future of shopping in India — from the rise of AI-powered discovery and video-first commerce to the growing role of conversational messaging in driving transactions. The post From Scroll to Chat to Cart: Trends Reshaping How India Shops appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: Introducing Instants: A New Way to Share in the Moment
entry.content: Share authentic moments as they’re happening with your Close Friends or mutual followers with just a tap. The post Introducing Instants: A New Way to Share in the Moment appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: Introducing a Completely Private Way to Chat With AI
entry.content: Incognito Chat lets you talk to Meta AI in a way that is invisible to anyone else. The post Introducing a Completely Private Way to Chat With AI appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: Reel Friends: Building Social Discovery that Scales to Billions
entry.content: On its face the new Friend Bubbles feature looks simple enough. It highlights Reels your friends have watched and reacted to. But sometimes the features that seem the most straightforward require the deepest engineering work. On this episode of the Meta Tech Podcast, Pascal Hartig chats with Subasree and Joseph, two software engineers from the Facebook [...] Read More... The post Reel Friends: Building Social Discovery that Scales to Billions appeared first on Engineering at Meta .

entry.title: Celebrating African Cinema With the ‘Made by Africa, Loved by the World’ 2026 Campaign
entry.content: In celebration of Africa Day on May 25, Facebook has launched the sixth edition of its pan-African campaign, ‘Made by Africa, loved by the world: Where stories spark community.’ This year’s campaign shifts the spotlight to African cinema. The 2026 campaign features a five-part vodcast series profiling five internationally acclaimed actors and filmmakers from Nigeria and South Africa, hosted by leading African podcasters I Said What I Said (Nigeria) and Because We Said So (South Africa). “For...

entry.title: Migrating Data Ingestion Systems at Meta Scale
entry.content: Meta’s data ingestion system, which our engineering teams leverage for up-to-date snapshots of the social graph, has recently undergone a significant revamp to enhance its reliability at scale. Moving from our legacy system to our new architecture required a large-scale migration of our entire data ingestion system. We’re sharing the solutions and strategies that enabled [...] Read More... The post Migrating Data Ingestion Systems at Meta Scale appeared first on Engineering at Meta .

entry.title: New Supervision Tools Give Parents Insights Into Their Teen’s Algorithm and More
entry.content: We’re making it easier for parents to manage and supervise their teen’s experiences across our apps. The post New Supervision Tools Give Parents Insights Into Their Teen’s Algorithm and More appeared first on Meta Newsroom .

entry.title: Save the Date: Meta Connect 2026
entry.content: Come join us at Connect from September 23–24 at our campus in Menlo Park, California, and get the first glimpse of what’s coming to the next computing platform.

entry.title: Labyrinth 1.1: Making End-to-End Encrypted Backups Even More Reliable
entry.content: We’re rolling out version 1.1 of Labyrinth, the encrypted storage system and protocol that secures messages and history on Messenger. Labyrinth 1.1 enhances the reliability of end-to-end encrypted backups with a new sub-protocol that helps messages survive the loss of a device, a switched device, and long gaps between sign-ins. Read our updated white paper, [...] Read More... The post Labyrinth 1.1: Making End-to-End Encrypted Backups Even More Reliable appeared first on Engineering at Meta .

entry.title: How Meta Is Strengthening End-to-End Encrypted Backups
entry.content: The HSM-based Backup Key Vault Meta’s HSM-based Backup Key Vault provides the foundation for end-to-end encrypted backups for WhatsApp and Messenger. The system allows people to protect their backed-up message history with a recovery code, ensuring that the recovery code is stored in tamper-resistant hardware security modules (HSMs) and is inaccessible to Meta, cloud storage [...] Read More... The post How Meta Is Strengthening End-to-End Encrypted Backups appeared first on Engineering at Meta .

entry.title: Modernizing the Facebook Groups Search to Unlock the Power of Community Knowledge
entry.content: We’ve fundamentally transformed Facebook Groups Search to help people more reliably discover, sort through, and validate community content that’s most relevant to them. We’ve adopted a new hybrid retrieval architecture and implemented automated model-based evaluation to address the major friction points people experience when searching community content. Under this new framework, we’ve made tangible improvements [...] Read More... The post Modernizing the Facebook Groups Search to Unlock the...

entry.title: Capacity Efficiency at Meta: How Unified AI Agents Optimize Performance at Hyperscale
entry.content: We’re sharing insights into Meta’s Capacity Efficiency Program, where we’ve built an AI agent platform that helps automate finding and fixing performance issues throughout our infrastructure. By leveraging encoded domain expertise across a unified, standardized tool interface these agents help save power and free up engineers’ time away from addressing performance issues to innovating on [...] Read More... The post Capacity Efficiency at Meta: How Unified AI Agents Optimize Performance at Hyp...

entry.title: Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration at Meta: Framework, Lessons, and Takeaways
entry.content: We’re sharing lessons learned from Meta’s post-quantum cryptography (PQC) migration to help other organizations strengthen their resilience as industry transitions to post-quantum cryptography standards. We’re proposing the idea of PQC Migration Levels to help teams within organizations manage the complexity of PQC migration for their various use cases. By outlining Meta’s approach to this work [...] Read More... The post Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration at Meta: Framework, Lessons, and Ta...

entry.title: Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases
entry.content: At Meta, WebRTC powers real-time audio and video across various platforms. But forking a large open-source project like WebRTC within our monorepo presents unique challenges – over time, an internal fork can drift behind upstream, cutting itself off from community upgrades. We’re sharing how we escaped this “forking trap” – from building a dual-stack architecture [...] Read More... The post Escaping the Fork: How Meta Modernized WebRTC Across 50+ Use Cases appeared first on Engineering at Meta .

entry.title: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence
entry.content: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence

entry.title: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI
entry.content: Scaling How We Build and Test Our Most Advanced AI

entry.title: How Alta Daily Uses Meta’s Segment Anything to Reimagine the Digital Closet
entry.content: How Alta Daily Uses Meta’s Segment Anything to Reimagine the Digital Closet

entry.title: SAM 3.1: Faster and More Accessible Real-Time Video Detection and Tracking With Multiplexing and Global Reasoning
entry.content: SAM 3.1: Faster and More Accessible Real-Time Video Detection and Tracking With Multiplexing and Global Reasoning

entry.title: Introducing TRIBE v2: A Predictive Foundation Model Trained to Understand How the Human Brain Processes Complex Stimuli
entry.content: Introducing TRIBE v2: A Predictive Foundation Model Trained to Understand How the Human Brain Processes Complex Stimuli

entry.title: Four MTIA Chips in Two Years: Scaling AI Experiences for Billions
entry.content: Four MTIA Chips in Two Years: Scaling AI Experiences for Billions

entry.title: Mapping the World's Forests with Greater Precision: Introducing Canopy Height Maps v2
entry.content: Today, in partnership with the World Resources Institute, we’re announcing Canopy Height Maps v2 (CHMv2): an open source model and world-scale maps generated with it.

entry.title: Reducing Government Costs and Increasing Access to Greenspaces in the United Kingdom with DINO
entry.content: Meta's DINOv2 model is enhancing reforestation efforts around the world. Learn how the UK government is using DINO to help reduce costs and increase access to greenspaces.

entry.title: How DINO and SAM are Helping Modernize Essential Medical Triage Practices
entry.content: By leveraging advanced AI models, teams at the University of Pennsylvania are aiming to bring cutting-edge automation to emergency response.

entry.title: The Universities Space Research Association Applies Segment Anything Model for Responding to Flood Emergencies
entry.content: The Universities Space Research Association and Meta are collaborating to help support water observing systems set up by the U.S. Geological Survey.

entry.title: Introducing SAM Audio: The First Unified Multimodal Model for Audio Separation
entry.content: SAM Audio transforms audio processing by making it easy to isolate any sound from complex audio mixtures using natural, multimodal prompts — whether through text, visual cues, or marking time segments.

entry.title: How Conservation X Labs Is Using Segment Anything Model 3 for Endangered Wildlife Monitoring
entry.content: Start-ups like Conservation X Labs are using Meta’s Segment Anything Models to bolster on-the-ground conservation expertise.

entry.title: ExecuTorch Adoption in Reality Labs: Powering On-Device AI Across Meta Devices
entry.content: ExecuTorch, Meta’s open source, lightweight, and efficient inference engine, has been instrumental in enabling on-device AI capabilities across Meta’s family of apps.

entry.title: Introducing SAM 3D: Powerful 3D Reconstruction for Physical World Images
entry.content: This release introduces two new state-of-the-art models: SAM 3D Objects for object and scene reconstruction, and SAM 3D Body for human body and shape estimation.

entry.title: Omnilingual ASR: Advancing Automatic Speech Recognition for 1,600+ Languages
entry.content: We’re introducing Meta Omnilingual Automatic Speech Recognition, a suite of models providing automatic speech recognition capabilities for over 1,600 languages.

entry.title: Agents Rule of Two: A Practical Approach to AI Agent Security
entry.content: Agents Rule of Two: A Practical Approach to AI Agent Security

entry.title: The Building Blocks of Agentic AI: From Kernels to Clusters
entry.content: At PyTorch Conference 2025 in San Francisco, we unveiled five new projects spanning kernel languages, distributed systems, reinforcement learning, agentic frameworks, and edge AI deployment.

entry.title: How Llama and Oracle are helping Instituto PROA kickstart careers for students in Brazil
entry.content: Instituto PROA, a nonprofit organization in Brazil, has transformed its job preparation process for young candidates by leveraging Llama and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

entry.title: DINOv3: Self-supervised learning for vision at unprecedented scale
entry.content: DINOv3 scales self-supervised learning for images to create universal vision backbones that achieve absolute state-of-the-art performance across diverse domains, including web and satellite imagery.

entry.title: Small robots, mighty vision: NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory's DINOv2-enabled robot rovers and the future of planetary exploration
entry.content: Using DINOv2, the team at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory built a convenient robot operating system interface for robotic tasks.

entry.title: How DINOv3 is helping World Resources Institute restore forests and farms globally
entry.content: WRI and the Bezos Earth Fund used DINOv3 to develop an algorithm to accurately count individual trees from drone and satellite imagery.

entry.title: How Llama helps Biofy Technologies in the fight against antibiotic resistance
entry.content: Brazil, the biotech company Biofy Technologies has developed a groundbreaking platform using Llama that reduces diagnostic time for antibiotic resistance from five days to less than four hours.

entry.title: How Upwork helps freelancers win more work with Llama
entry.content: Upwork, one of the world’s largest work marketplaces, is using Llama to power Uma, its mindful AI, to help freelancers land jobs faster and more confidently.

entry.title: Joining forces with AWS on a new program to help startups build with Llama
entry.content: We're joining forces with Amazon Web Services to announce a new program that will provide resources and support to 30 promising startups in the U.S. that are building with Llama.

entry.title: Modeling natural conversational dynamics with Seamless Interaction
entry.content: Modeling natural conversational dynamics with Seamless Interaction

entry.title: How Llama helps drive engineering efficiency at a major Australian bank
entry.content: ANZ, one of Australia's Big Four banks, is driving engineering efficiency with Llama.

entry.title: Announcing the inaugural Llama Startup Program cohort
entry.content: At Meta, we believe in the potential of early-stage startups to drive innovation in the generative AI market, and through the Llama Startup Program, we aim to lower the barrier to entry for getting started with Llama models.

entry.title: Introducing the V-JEPA 2 world model and new benchmarks for physical reasoning
entry.content: We’re excited to share V-JEPA 2, the first world model trained on video that enables state-of-the-art understanding and prediction, as well as zero-shot planning and robot control in new environments.

entry.title: Inside Aria Gen 2: Explore the cutting-edge tech behind the device
entry.content: Today, we’re excited to share more about the technology inside Aria Gen 2. This includes an in-depth overview of the form factor, audio capabilities, battery life, upgraded cameras and sensors, on-device compute, and more.

entry.title: Introducing the Llama Startup Program
entry.content: We’re excited to announce the Llama Startup Program, a new initiative to empower early stage startups to innovate and build generative AI applications with Llama.

entry.title: Sharing new breakthroughs and artifacts supporting molecular property prediction, language processing, and neuroscience
entry.content: Meta FAIR is sharing new research artifacts that highlight our commitment to advanced machine intelligence (AMI) through focused scientific and academic progress.

entry.title: Meet the winners of our first-ever LlamaCon Hackathon
entry.content: The challenge was to create a demonstrable project using the Llama API, Llama 4 Scout, or Llama 4 Maverick—or any combination of these cutting-edge tools—within just 24 hours.

entry.title: How Common Sense Machines uses Meta Segment Anything Model and AI to generate production-ready 3D assets
entry.content: Common Sense Machines uses Meta Segment Anything Model 2 to analyze 2D images and video and translate their components to 3D.

entry.title: How Meta Segment Anything Model enables Cutouts in the Instagram Edits app
entry.content: People can use Cutouts to edit across several layers of video, applying filters to specific parts of videos and easily place elements like text and stickers behind objects.

entry.title: Sharing new open source protection tools and advancements in AI privacy and security
entry.content: Today, we’re releasing new Llama protection tools for the open source AI community.

entry.title: Everything we announced at our first-ever LlamaCon
entry.content: Here’s a look at what we announced at LlamaCon and how you can get started with our newest releases.

entry.title: How Litmos is using Llama to make corporate learning more intuitive
entry.content: With Llama 3.1 8B, Litmos created an AI-powered assistant that enables natural language search, making the LMS more intuitive and user-friendly.

entry.title: Advancing AI systems through progress in perception, localization, and reasoning
entry.content: Meta FAIR is releasing several new research artifacts that advance our understanding of perception and support our goal of achieving advanced machine intelligence (AMI).

entry.title: The Llama 4 herd: The beginning of a new era of natively multimodal AI innovation
entry.content: We’re introducing Llama 4 Scout and Llama 4 Maverick, the first open-weight natively multimodal models with unprecedented context support and our first built using a mixture-of-experts (MoE) architecture.

entry.title: How Tavus is helping to make AI videos feel like real conversations
entry.content: Tavus, an AI video research company, leverages advanced AI models to create human-like digital interactions that feel as authentic as real human conversations.

entry.title: How Cornerstone is transforming XR training to build essential skills for the modern workforce
entry.content: Cornerstone is transforming how employees learn. Using Llama, they’re taking that experience to the next level through extended reality training scenarios.

entry.title: Our open source Llama models are helping to spur economic growth in the US
entry.content: We’ve shared how Llama’s impact has already been felt by businesses and entrepreneurs. And today, we’re looking at how Llama is helping to spur economic growth in the US.

entry.title: Meta at SXSW: How Llama and open source AI is leveling the playing field and enabling innovation
entry.content: At SXSW, Meta and Llama developers showcased the impact of open source AI, and participated in policy discussions and hands-on demos.

entry.title: How Smartly is improving customer service and automating technical support with generative AI
entry.content: Smartly, a digital advertising platform, harnesses the power of AI to scale ads across channels.

entry.title: Building foundation models for understanding human pathology using DINOv2
entry.content: When Dr. Faisal Mahmood set up his laboratory at the intersection of pathology and machine learning. The team credits open source models, including Meta’s DINO, with helping them do their work.

entry.title: How Sofya is taking clinical reasoning to the next level with Llama
entry.content: Sofya turned to Llama with the goal of fostering the development of healthcare solutions in Brazil and Latin America.

entry.title: How Sevilla FC is discovering future soccer stars with Llama
entry.content: Sevilla FC’s data department partnered with IBM to create Scout Advisor, a generative AI-driven scouting tool designed and deployed on watsonx, with Llama 3.1 70B Instruct.

entry.title: How Orakl Oncology is using DINOv2 to accelerate cancer treatment discovery
entry.content: Orakl Oncology aims to accelerate cancer research and drug development by combining experimental, lab-based insights and machine learning.

entry.title: Dramatically accelerating patient-matching in clinical trials with open source
entry.content: Mendel AI’s flagship product, Mendel Hypercube, tackles clinical tasks. With Llama, Hypercube can also be used for trial matching and putting together patient groups.

entry.title: EgoMimic: Georgia Tech PhD student uses Project Aria Research Glasses to help train humanoid robots
entry.content: Today, we’re highlighting new research from Georgia Tech that helps train robots to perform basic everyday tasks using egocentric recordings from wearers of Meta’s Project Aria research glasses.

entry.title: Reimagining K-12 Education: How Blended Labs is raising the bar with AI-driven schools
entry.content: Blended, an educational technology and AI company based in Germany, is using Llama 3.1 as the foundation of its flagship product, Blended OS, an AI-native school operating system.

entry.title: Update: Expanding access to Meta Segment Anything 2.1 on Amazon SageMaker JumpStart
entry.content: Starting today, SAM 2.1 is available in Amazon SageMaker JumpStart, making it easier than ever to deploy SAM 2.1 and integrate it into new applications and workflows.

entry.title: AI Artifacts: An interview with Ruben Fro and Benjamin Bardou
entry.content: In honor of this year’s AI Action Summit, we partnered with the Bibliothèque Nationale de France (BnF), Fisheye Immersive, Convergence, and Institut Montaigne to commission two open source AI-generated art installations by innovative artists, Ruben Fro and Benjamin Bardou.

entry.title: Advancing machine intelligence through human-centered research
entry.content: Meta FAIR is sharing some of our most recent research and models that support our goal of achieving AMI, and our long-held commitment to sharing open and reproducible science.

entry.title: Using AI to decode language from the brain and advance our understanding of human communication
entry.content: Today, in collaboration with the Basque Center on Cognition, Brain and Language, we’re excited to share two breakthroughs that show how AI can help advance our understanding of human intelligence, leading us closer to AMI.

entry.title: Enlisting Llama in India’s first open source audio language model
entry.content: Sarvam used Llama to develop enterprise voice AI agents with improved reasoning capabilities that are proficient in 10 Indian languages.

entry.title: How Altana employs Llama to elevate global value chain management
entry.content: Altana uses AI to help businesses and governments manage their global supply chains, providing insight into extended supplier and distribution networks.

entry.title: How BrightHeart uses Meta’s DINOv2 to transform fetal heart screenings
entry.content: BrightHeart has created an AI-powered medical software with the support of Meta’s DINOv2 model to help clinicians identify or rule out signs suggestive of congenital heart defects.

entry.title: Scaling an AI coding assistant built with Llama to hundreds of thousands of users
entry.content: Codeium’s AI-enabled coding assistant leverages Llama for its free and premiere offerings.

entry.title: How digital artist Josephine Miller uses Meta Segment Anything to help design the future of fashion
entry.content: Josephine, a London-based XR creative designer and content creator, takes us behind the scenes of how she uses open source technology in her work.

entry.title: How Llama is helping Saama deliver new possibilities in personalized medicine and data-driven care
entry.content: OpenBioLLM, a series of fine-tuned Llama models from life sciences company Saama, streamlines tasks that can accelerate clinical trials and potentially create new possibilities in personalized medicine.

entry.title: How Virgo is using DINOv2 to analyze endoscopy videos for precision medicine
entry.content: Virgo, a company based in San Diego, California, believes endoscopy video and AI are a powerful combination that could lead to improved patient outcomes.

entry.title: How TU Dresden is advancing precision oncology and transforming healthcare AI
entry.content: TUD Dresden University of Technology’s Clinical AI research group is transforming cancer care through computational innovation, focusing on precision oncology.

entry.title: Finding the perfect book to read next with a Llama-based assistant
entry.content: Scribd, Inc.’s Everand reading service is home to a global library of millions of ebooks, audiobooks and more.

entry.title: Introducing Nymeria, a dataset for improving human motion prediction for AR and VR devices
entry.content: The Nymeria dataset provides egocentric human motion in the wild at an unprecedented scale, capturing a broad spectrum of people engaging in everyday activities across varied locations.

entry.title: The future of AI: Built with Llama
entry.content: As we close out 2024, Meta is leading the industry forward in AI product and technology experiences and setting a new standard for how the industry builds and advances AI.

entry.title: How Spotify is using Llama to create personalized recommendations and enhance content discovery
entry.content: Spotify uses Llama to deliver contextualized recommendations to boost artist discovery and create an even richer user experience.

entry.title: Sharing new research, models, and datasets from Meta FAIR
entry.content: Meta FAIR is releasing new research artifacts that highlight our recent innovations in developing agents, robustness and safety, and architectures that facilitate machine learning.

entry.title: How Inarix is using DINOv2 to revolutionize the agricultural supply chain
entry.content: Inarix is transforming the agricultural industry by turning smartphones into pocket laboratories using Meta FAIR’s DinoV2.
